text:
Code reviews are an essential practice for any development team. One of their main benefits is catching bugs early in the development process. When a developer reviews a teammate's code, they can spot mistakes or logic errors that the original author might have missed. This saves time and effort later.

Beyond finding bugs, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing among team members. Developers learn new techniques, coding standards, and different approaches to solving problems. This sharing helps everyone improve their skills over time.

Ultimately, regular code reviews boost overall team quality. The team produces cleaner, more reliable code, and everyone becomes more confident in their work.
